channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Russia Targets Ukraine In Overnight Drone Attack, Parts Found In Romania
item.description: Russian drones crashed in Ukraine and Romania, prompting evacuations and a police cordon in Galati. The incident marks the latest escalation in the ongoing conflict between Russia and Ukraine. Details of the attack remain unclear.

item.title: Tottenham and West Ham both win, as heated battle to avoid relegation continues
item.description: Tottenham and West Ham both won their respective matches, maintaining their positions in the Premier League relegation battle. The victories came as a result of strong performances from both teams, with Tottenham defeating Southampton 3-1 and West Ham beating Everton 2-0.

item.title: Pentagon Floats Suspending Spain From NATO in Leaked Email Fallout
item.description: The Pentagon considered suspending Spain from NATO membership in a leaked email, citing concerns over the country's military cooperation with Russia. The proposal was reportedly made in response to Spain's decision to purchase Russian-made military equipment.

item.title: Britain courts Trump with King Charles
item.description: King Charles met with former US President Donald Trump during a state visit to the US, aiming to repair strained US-UK relations. The British monarch's visit comes as the two nations seek to strengthen their diplomatic ties and address shared concerns.

item.title: Trump Cancels Top Officials' Pakistan Trip For Iran Talks
item.description: US officials cancelled a trip to Pakistan for Iran talks, hours after Iranian Foreign Minister departed Islamabad. The decision was made by former US President Donald Trump, who had planned to send a delegation to Pakistan for diplomatic discussions with Iran.

item.title: US to allow Venezuelan government to cover Maduro’s lawyer fees
item.description: The US will cover Venezuelan President Maduro's lawyer fees. Defence lawyers claimed Maduro's rights were violated after a US abduction. Defence lawyers had asked for case to be thrown out, claiming Maduro's rights were violated following US abduction.

item.title: Palhinha scores late Tottenham goal at Wolves to end winless run
item.description: Palhinha scored a late goal for Tottenham at Wolves. The win ended Tottenham's winless run. Palhinha scores late Tottenham goal at Wolves to end winless run.

item.title: Wilson's late winner boosts West Ham's survival chances with 2-1 win over Everton
item.description: Wilson scored a late winner for West Ham against Everton. The 2-1 win boosted West Ham's survival chances. Wilson's late winner boosts West Ham's survival chances with 2-1 win over Everton.

item.title: Punjab pull off record chase against Delhi despite Rahul ton
item.description: Punjab pulled off a record chase against Delhi in a cricket match despite a century from Rahul. The team's victory was a surprise upset in a highly anticipated match. Punjab pull off record chase against Delhi despite Rahul ton.

item.title: Olise and Kane lead Bayern comeback from three goals down in 4-3 win at Mainz
item.description: Olise and Kane led Bayern's comeback against Mainz. Bayern won 4-3 after being three goals down. Olise and Kane lead Bayern comeback from three goals down in 4-3 win at Mainz.

item.title: Taliban Government says Afghans in Qatar can return 'with full confidence'
item.description: The Taliban government told Afghans in Qatar they can return with full confidence. The US aims to move Afghans to a third country. A U. S.

item.title: Indigenous Speakers Are Booed During Commemorations of Australia’s War Dead
item.description: Australia's Anzac Day dawn services were disrupted in three cities by hecklers targeting Aboriginal customs. Dawn services for Anzac Day were disrupted in three cities. The hecklers seemed to be targeting a widespread Aboriginal custom meant to acknowledge the land’s original inhabitants.

item.title: Explosions and gunfire as armed groups launch co-ordinated attacks across Mali
item.description: Armed groups launched co-ordinated attacks across Mali, sparking explosions and gunfire in the centre and north. The attacks are described as the largest jihadist assault in years, with witnesses reporting clashes.

item.title: Iran’s Foreign Minister leaves Islamabad without meeting U.S. envoys, say Pakistan officials
item.description: Iran's Foreign Minister met Pakistan's Army chief and resumed commercial flights from Tehran's airport. Iran resumes commercial flights from Tehran’s international airport for the first time since the conflict with the U. S.

item.title: Japan’s New Care Workers: Bodybuilders, Wrestlers and M.M.A. Fighters
item.description: Japan recruits athletes to work in nursing homes due to staff shortages. Young athletes are being recruited to plug a staff shortage in Japan’s nursing homes. The results are reshaping elder care.

item.title: Beijing Warns Europe As Chinese Entities Included In Russia Sanctions
item.description: China demands removal of its entities from Russia sanctions lists. China demanded an immediate removal of Chinese entities from the sanctions list. Beijing Warns Europe As Chinese Entities Included In Russia Sanctions.

item.title: Paris and Athens exchange mutual defense pledges amid geopolitical turmoil - politico.eu
item.description: Paris and Athens exchanged mutual defense pledges. Paris and Athens exchange mutual defense pledges amid geopolitical turmoil politico. eu. Paris and Athens exchange mutual defense pledges amid geopolitical turmoil - politico. eu.

item.title: Mali hit by wave of coordinated attacks from armed groups
item.description: Mali's capital Bamako was hit by coordinated attacks from armed groups. Gunfire and explosions have rocked Mali's capital Bamako and other key cities in one of the most significant coordinated attacks in years, as armed groups, including jihadist insurgents and separatist rebels exploit worsening insecurity in the Sahel region.

item.title: Brazil's Militao risks missing World Cup with hamstring injury, sources say
item.description: Brazil's Eder Militao may miss the World Cup due to a hamstring injury. Sources indicate the injury could keep him out of the tournament. Brazil's Militao risks missing World Cup with hamstring injury, sources say.

item.title: African governments need to take urgent action on fertiliser shortages
item.description: Fertiliser shortages threaten African harvests due to slowed shipments from the Strait of Hormuz. Governments face urgent calls to address the issue. Tensions in the Strait of Hormuz have slowed fertiliser shipments which could put harvests at risk.
